case_name: Georgia Ann Miller v. Tran, M.D. Trinh

summary.brief: Georgia Ann Miller v. Tran, M.D. Trinh. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: Georgia Miller brought a medical malpractice action against Dr. Trinh Tran and Dr. Tran’s employer, Allcare Rheumatology (“Allcare”).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On June 25, 2019, Miller filed a medical malpractice action against Dr. Tran and Allcare.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Miller had her first visit with Dr. Tran on February 20, 2015.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Tran recommended that Miller begin taking hydroxychloroquine and a steroid—prednisone.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Miller reported reduced pain since beginning the steroids, but her pain did not completely abate.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In October 2015, Dr. Tranh changed the prescription to Rayos, a slow-releasing form of prednisone, to minimize side effects.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On December 18, 2015, Miller reported increased pain and flare-ups. She had torn a biceps tendon near the shoulder when reaching back.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Laboratory testing in December 2016 revealed a Vectra score of forty-five, which indicated high rheumatoid arthritis disease activity.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Miller sought a second opinion regarding her symptoms from the Cleveland Clinic.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Littlejohn did not believe there was sufficient evidence that Miller had lupus or a form of arthritis.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Dr. Gregori then offered his expert opinion that Dr. Tran’s treatment caused Miller’s injuries.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The jury found Dr. Tran and Allcare not liable.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court did not abuse its discretion in instructing the jury on the Choice of Treatment Modalities instruction.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Years of pain in muscles and joints, swelling in knuckles, stiffness, pain in shoulders and hips, hair loss, weight gain, mouth sores, and rashes
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Referred to Dr. Tran by primary care physician due to suspected rheumatological disease. Blood testing revealed ANA levels four times above normal.

focused_exam.joints: Tenderness, swelling, and synovial thickening in several joints

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Suspected inflammatory disease such as rheumatoid arthritis, polymyalgia rheumatica, or lupus

reveal.result_summary: ANA levels four times above normal
reveal.decision_impact: Supports further investigation for inflammatory diseases

requestables.name: Vectra Score
requestables.description: Blood test to assess rheumatoid arthritis disease activity

reveal.result_summary: Vectra score of forty-five indicating high rheumatoid arthritis disease activity
reveal.decision_impact: Supports continuation of treatment for inflammatory disease

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient referred to Dr. Tran for suspected rheumatological disease

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Treatment
timeline_phases.description: Ongoing treatment with steroids and other medications

key_events.event: Change to Rayos and addition of methotrexate
Laboratory testing reveals high Vectra score

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient experiencing ongoing symptoms despite treatment
decision_points.prompt: Should the steroid treatment be continued?

options.clinical_reasoning: Symptoms could be due to fibromyalgia; long-term steroid use not justified

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed symptoms were due to inflammatory disease

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert opinion suggested reevaluation of treatment

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Continued steroid use despite lack of improvement
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Reevaluation and possible discontinuation of steroids

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced tendon injuries and other symptoms
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Chronic pain managed with non-steroid medications

key_findings: Dr. Tran's treatment did not cause the injuries
Jury found Dr. Tran and Allcare not liable

factual_timeline.event: Vectra score revealed high disease activity

court_findings.finding: Dr. Tran's treatment did not cause the injuries
